primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: Four segments with distinct growth, margin, risk, and correct valuation method. CONTRACT §2.1: decompose into real economic segments, value each on its own terms.

segments.name: P&D — Power and Discrete (SiC MOSFET, silicon power)
segments.reasoning: Cyclical segment near the trough. METHOD: normalized mid-cycle EBITDA × through-cycle EV/EBITDA multiple. The exit-multiple method would reward the trough-to-normalized climb which is already largely priced in by the market. The through-cycle multiple on mid-cycle earnings is the CONTRACT §2.2 'cyclical' prescription. Peers Infineon and Onsemi trade at 8-11x EV/EBITDA through the cycle; STM's P&D is lower-quality (more commodity SiC, less diversification) so apply 8.5x. WACC: β 1.35 (cyclical, high SiC concentration, Chinese competition exposure) → Ke 10.6% → WACC 10.1% (modest net cash in segment allocation).

multiple_inputs.multiple_rationale: Mid-cycle EBITDA estimate for P&D: $2.7B mid-cycle revenue x 29% EBITDA margin (Infineon auto/power segment reference) = $783M. Through-cycle EV/EBITDA of 8.5x anchored to Infineon (9x) and Onsemi (8x) at similar cycle positions; 8.5x reflects STM's lower diversification and higher China SiC competition risk. Net debt allocation: zero (group level). WACC: 10.1%.
multiple_inputs.argument: Mid-cycle P&D revenue ~$2.7B (below prior peak $3.1B in 2024; STM has lost SiC share). EBITDA margin 29% = $783M. 8.5x EV/EBITDA = $6.7B EV. This is a conservative-but-not-pessimistic mid-cycle multiple; Infineon's auto segment trades at 9-10x. The bear on SiC (2-3 years of overcapacity) is already reflected in the below-peak mid-cycle revenue assumption.

segments.name: EMP — Embedded Processing (STM32 + automotive MCU)
segments.reasoning: STM32 is a mature-but-growing franchise with strong ecosystem stickiness. The MCU market grows at ~5% CAGR per IHS Markit. STM32 grows faster due to industrial IoT and edge AI content growth. Method: NTM revenue × EV/sales multiple (correct for a semiconductor franchise where sales are the stable anchor; EV/EBITDA would amplify the trough distortion). EV/sales of 3.5x anchored to NXP's EMP-comparable segments and Microchip Technology (3-4x). WACC: β 1.15 (stable franchise, lower cyclicality than SiC) → Ke 9.7% → WACC 9.5%.

multiple_inputs.multiple_rationale: NTM EMP revenue: $4.1B (FY2026 estimate; EMP grew +31% YoY in Q1 2026 and we project +15% for FY2026 on the industrial restocking cycle). EV/Sales 3.5x: comparable to NXP auto/MCU segments (3.0-4.0x) and Microchip Technology (3.5-4.0x). STM32 ecosystem stickiness and 800+ device variants justify the upper end of the range vs. commodity MCU makers. WACC: 9.5%.
multiple_inputs.argument: EMP is the most defensible segment — STM32 ecosystem lock-in, automotive MCU design wins, and industrial IoT tailwind. $4.1B NTM revenue at 3.5x EV/Sales = $14.35B EV. Revenue growing at 13-15% through recovery; 3.5x is not aggressive for a franchise-quality MCU platform.

segments.name: AM&S — Analog, MEMS and Sensors
segments.reasoning: Mature franchise with modest growth (10% CAGR driven mainly by NXP MEMS acquisition contribution). Method: EV/sales multiple. Apple MEMS relationship is durable but not fast-growing. MEMS sensor leadership in inertial sensors is defensible. EV/Sales 3.0x: below TI analog (7-8x) reflecting STM's higher cyclicality and Apple concentration; above a pure commodity analog (2x). WACC: β 1.10 (lower than P&D; Apple relationship is relatively stable; NXP MEMS adds automotive diversification) → Ke 9.5% → WACC 9.3%.

multiple_inputs.multiple_rationale: NTM AM&S revenue: $5.3B (FY2026 estimate; NXP MEMS contribution + Apple recovery). EV/Sales 3.0x: conservative relative to TI analog (7x) but TI has a far more diversified customer base. STM's Apple concentration (12.3% of company revenue) and cyclical personal electronics exposure warrant a discount. WACC: 9.3%.
multiple_inputs.argument: AM&S: $5.3B NTM revenue at 3.0x EV/Sales = $15.9B EV. The NXP MEMS acquisition adds scale and margin accretion. Apple iPhone 18 LiDAR win (unconfirmed) would be upside. 3.0x is calibrated to STM's customer concentration and semi-cyclical nature.

segments.name: RF&OC — RF and Optical Communications (Satellite + Data Center)
segments.reasoning: RF&OC is the fastest-growing segment — from $1.4B in FY2025 to an implied $4.4B by 2028. It is driven by two structurally contracted or near-contracted revenue streams: Starlink (~90% share, ongoing production orders) and the AWS multi-year agreement. The exit-multiple method is correct for a high-growth segment per CONTRACT §2.2: build NOPAT to a normalization year (2028) when growth matures, apply the Gordon steady-state multiple, discount at segment WACC. WACC: β 1.20 (higher than group average — new, unproven segment revenue from AWS; satellite has some SpaceX concentration risk; but partially offset by the contracted nature of Starlink) → Ke 9.9% → WACC 9.7%.

exit_inputs.reasoning: NOPAT at normalization year 2028 (T=3): RF&OC revenue $4.4B (base) x 20% NOPAT margin = $880M. NOPAT margin of 20%: satellite chips are high-margin (Starlink is captive, high-ASP RF ICs); data center silicon photonics is early and margin expansion is in progress. 20% NOPAT margin is below mature analog comps (25-30%) but appropriate for a ramp phase. G_lt = 7%: long-term growth of satellite + AI interconnect is higher than industrial semi but below current ramp; 7% captures the mature run-rate after the initial Kuiper + AI build-out (2028-2032). ROIC = 28%: capital-light relative to IDM peers (RF&OC uses existing fab capacity; no dedicated new fab required). r = 9.7%: WACC for this segment. Exit multiple (Gordon): (1-0.07/0.28)/(0.097-0.07) = (1-0.25)/0.027 = 0.75/0.027 = 27.8x. Discounted to today: $880M x 27.8x / (1.097)^3 = $24.4B / 1.32 = $18.5B. Plus interim FCF PV $150M = ~$18.7B EV for RF&OC.
exit_inputs.exit_multiple_derivation: Gordon: (1 - g/ROIC)/(r - g) = (1 - 0.07/0.28) / (0.097 - 0.07) = 0.75 / 0.027 = 27.8x. Sanity check: mature optical/satellite component peers (Coherent, MACOM) trade at 20-25x EBITDA; 27.8x EV/NOPAT is slightly above on a NOPAT basis, reflecting the satellite moat. If ROIC = 25% (more conservative), exit multiple = (1-0.07/0.25)/(0.097-0.07) = 0.72/0.027 = 26.7x — minimal sensitivity.
exit_inputs.t_invariance_note: If normalization year is 2029 (T=4) with NOPAT ~$1.04B: $1.04B x 27.8x / (1.097)^4 = $28.9B / 1.45 = $19.9B. If 2027 (T=2) with NOPAT ~$0.62B: $0.62B x 27.8x / (1.097)^2 = $17.2B / 1.20 = $14.4B. The T-invariance range $14.4B to $19.9B is wide — this reflects genuine uncertainty in the data center ramp path. Base case uses T=3 ($18.7B EV). This is moderate-to-wide T-sensitivity, consistent with MEDIUM conviction on this segment.

earnings_bridge.bridge_note: NTM EPS $2.46 (street consensus, non-GAAP influenced) + $0.25 for excess D&A vs. capex at current low-utilization (capex normalizing down from FY2022's $3.6B). SBC net dilution haircut $0.15 (STM has modest SBC program; convertible bonds add minimal dilution at current price). Owner FCF/share $1.80 reflects capex is still elevated as 200mm SiC conversion continues — FCF suppressed in 2026-2027 but normalizes to $3.00+ by 2028 as the capex cycle completes.

blending_logic: Pure SOTP — sum of segment EVs less parent net debt
